text: Worked through reframing the openreview tool from a single-purpose systematic-literature-review (SLR) pipeline into a dual-mode system supporting both SLRs and deep-research runs, starting from the observation that the README/dashboard read as a general PubMed search tool and that the extraction skill had been hardcoded for one specific study. Did an impact analysis, then implemented the two-mode split with separate directories per study type, refactored repo.py to add a "reviews" constant symmetric to the existing "studies" constant (removing the asymmetry the user flagged), and rebuilt the dashboard to serve both run types under one shared view with a corrected study/run count in the header. Updated the README to match the two-mode framing and rolled the FASHA systematic review forward live while the user watched the dashboard. Diagnosed why the melatonin deep-research run's step-six synthesis silently failed to complete and went undetected, then agreed to fix the detection gap and delete the stale melatonin research data.
